### Tickets by Request Type

The number of PSOM Jira Service Management tickets in the latest snapshot
grouped by JSM request type, showing which categories of service requests are
most common.

*Alternative names*:

- service request type breakdown
- PSOM tickets by request type
- request category distribution

**Calculation.** Filter to the most recent `date` partition and count tickets by
`request_type`. Tickets that are not JSM service requests will have an empty
`request_type`; these are counted separately as `(non-service-request)`.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## SQL

```sql
SELECT
    CASE WHEN request_type = '' THEN '(non-service-request)' ELSE request_type END AS request_type,
    COUNT(*) AS ticket_count
FROM jira.psom
WHERE date = (SELECT MAX(date) FROM jira.psom)
GROUP BY request_type
ORDER BY ticket_count DESC;
```

## R

```r
psom <- tbl(con, in_schema("jira", "psom"))
latest_date <- psom |> summarise(d = max(date, na.rm = TRUE)) |> pull(d)

psom |>
  filter(as.Date(date) == as.Date(latest_date)) |>
  mutate(request_type = if_else(request_type == "", "(non-service-request)", request_type)) |>
  count(request_type, name = "ticket_count") |>
  arrange(desc(ticket_count))
```

:::
